mission.title: metallb: Replace the MetalLB configmap with k8s custom resources
mission.description: Replace the MetalLB configmap with k8s custom resources. Requested by 11+ users.

steps.title: Check current metallb deployment
steps.description:
Verify your metallb version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n metallb-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metallb
helm list -n metallb-system 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working metallb installation.

steps.title: Review metallb configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant metallb configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n metallb-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metallb
kubectl get configmap -n metallb-system -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=metallb
```
MetalLB currently uses a ConfigMap for its configuration. This is easy, but not very kubernetes-native.

We should consider migrating the config to custom resource definitions.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Replace the MetalLB configmap with k8s custom resources
steps.description:
This design document discusses implementing MetalLB Custom Resource definition"CRD"
as a mechanism to configure MetalLB's layer2 and BGP features instead of using ConfigMap.

ran mdl against the new md file
```
mdl 0001-metallb-crd.md 
```
```yaml
apiVersion: ipcontroller.ext/v1
kind: IpClaimPool
metadata:
    name: test-pool
spec:
    cidr: 192.168.0.248/29
    ranges:
        - - 192.168.0.249
          - 192.168.0.250
        - - 192.168.0.252
          - 192.168.0.253
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n metallb-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metallb
kubectl get events -n metallb-system --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Replace the MetalLB configmap with k8s custom resources" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This design document discusses implementing MetalLB Custom Resource definition"CRD"
as a mechanism to configure MetalLB's layer2 and BGP features instead of using ConfigMap.

ran mdl against the new md file
```
mdl 0001-metallb-crd.md 
```
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: ipcontroller.ext/v1
kind: IpClaimPool
metadata:
    name: test-pool
spec:
    cidr: 192.168.0.248/29
    ranges:
        - - 192.168.0.249
          - 192.168.0.250
        - - 192.168.0.252
          - 192.168.0.253

apiVersion: metallb.universe.tf/v1
kind: AddressPool
metadata:
  name: my-addr-pool
spec:
  protocol: layer2
  cidr:
  - 192.168.16.240/30

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with metallb installed or the issue environment reproducible.
